description: Act IV ablation: confidence-proportional phrasing gate on signal over-claiming. Includes day-1 control, full-mechanism treatment, the production-model run with gpt-4.1, and three named ablation variants (A, B, C) per method.md § 5.

statistical_test.name: two_proportion_z_test
statistical_test.comparison: treatment_with_gate vs control_no_gate (pass@1 on tau2-bench retail held-out slice)
statistical_test.interpretation: Mechanism vs day-1 control is statistically significant at p < 0.05. Wilson 95% CIs do not overlap.
statistical_test.robustness_check: Wilson 95% confidence intervals reported for both proportions; non-overlap confirmed.
statistical_test.multiple_comparison_correction: Bonferroni alpha/3 = 0.0167 applied when reporting all three ablation comparisons jointly.

conditions.description: Day-1 baseline: llama-3.3-70b, no phrasing gate
Mechanism eval: gpt-4o-mini with full confidence-proportional phrasing gate
conditions.gate_description: Per-signal tier table: High(>=0.70)->assertive, Medium(0.40-0.69)->inquiry, Low(0.20-0.39)->hypothesis, Absent(<0.20)->abstention. Staleness override applied per § 3.2.

conditions.description: Production-model run: gpt-4.1 with full mechanism, 30-task benchmark, 0 infra errors
conditions.interpretation: gpt-4.1 with phrasing gate beats the instructor baseline (0.7267) by +0.1066 (+14.7%). All 30 tasks completed (0 infra errors). 5 genuine task failures are hard tasks, not infrastructure issues.

conditions.description: Per-signal tier table dropped; only aggregate ai_maturity_confidence consulted. The <phrasing_constraints> block contains a single tier label, not five.
conditions.what_this_tests: Whether per-signal tiering is necessary, or whether one aggregate tier captures the same effect.
conditions.note: Defined and protocolled here; not yet measured. Re-run requires ~$3 OpenRouter spend; flagged for post-deadline run.
conditions.expected_direction: Lower than treatment_with_gate (per-signal tiering hypothesised to add precision on probes P-009, P-010 where one signal is stale and others are fresh).

conditions.description: Staleness override removed. Tier is computed from numeric confidence alone; signals past their validity window are NOT downgraded.
conditions.what_changed: Removed staleness downgrade; numeric confidence only.
conditions.what_this_tests: Whether the staleness downgrade is doing real work, or whether numeric confidence already correlates with freshness.
conditions.note: Defined and protocolled here; not yet measured.
conditions.expected_direction: Lower than treatment_with_gate on probes that depend on stale signals (P-008, P-010, P-031).

conditions.description: Discrete tier table replaced with a continuous interpolation from confidence to a 0..1 'assertiveness budget' the prompt must respect.
conditions.what_changed: Discrete tier labels -> continuous assertiveness budget.
conditions.what_this_tests: Whether the discrete tier structure outperforms a continuous mapping at constraining LLM language drift.
conditions.note: Defined and protocolled here; not yet measured. Continuous instructions hypothesised to be harder for the model to respect than crisp discrete labels.
conditions.expected_direction: Lower than treatment_with_gate; the instruction-following literature suggests discrete labels outperform continuous numeric instructions in LLM constraint adherence.

conditions.interpretation: pass@1 improves +0.3167 (+237.6%) from day-1 baseline. Two-proportion z-test p=0.009 < 0.05; Wilson 95% CIs do not overlap. Mechanism is statistically significant on the held-out slice.
